title: Aberdeen Ward 7 heads to by-election after ANC councillor resigns
description: ABERDEEN NUUS - The Dr Beyers Naudé...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"ABERDEEN NUUS - The Dr Beyers Naudé Local Municipality (DBNLM)is heading towards a municipal by-election scheduled for 18 March.","html":"ABERDEEN NUUS - The Dr Beyers Naudé Local Municipality (DBNLM)is heading towards a municipal by-election scheduled for 18 March."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"This follows the recent resignation of ANC councillor Nathan Jacobs, which resulted in the Ward 7 - Aberdeen Town, Adendorp and Koebergville - ward councillor position being vacant."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"When a councillor resigns from a municipal council, the vacancy is dealt with in terms of the Local Government: Municipal Structures Act and the Electoral Act.","html":"When a councillor resigns from a municipal council, the vacancy is dealt with in terms of the Local Government: Municipal Structures Act and the Electoral Act."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"If the vacancy concerns a ward councillor, as is the case with Jacobs, a by-election must be held within 90 days of the date on which the vacancy arises so that voters can elect a new representative.","html":"If the vacancy concerns a ward councillor, as is the case with Jacobs, a by-election must be held within 90 days of the date on which the vacancy arises so that voters can elect a new representative."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"If it is a proportional representation (PR) councillor, the vacancy is filled by the relevant political party in accordance with its approved party list.","html":"If it is a proportional representation (PR) councillor, the vacancy is filled by the relevant political party in accordance with its approved party list."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Until the vacancy is filled, the council continues to function with one fewer member, and its decisions remain valid, provided that a quorum is present.","html":"Until the vacancy is filled, the council continues to function with one fewer member, and its decisions remain valid, provided that a quorum is present."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"For a municipal ward by-election in South Africa, only voters who are registered in the specific ward where the by-election is taking place are eligible to vote, while those registered in different wards or municipalities are not.","html":"For a municipal ward by-election in South Africa, only voters who are registered in the specific ward where the by-election is taking place are eligible to vote, while those registered in different wards or municipalities are not."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Any political party that is registered with the Electoral Commission of South Africa (IEC) is allowed to contest a municipal ward by-election."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In addition, independent candidates who meet the IEC’s requirements (for example, being South African citizens, being 18 years or older, and being residents within the particular municipality) may also make themselves available.","html":"In addition, independent candidates who meet the IEC’s requirements (for example, being South African citizens, being 18 years or older, and being residents within the particular municipality) may also make themselves available."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In this event, the IEC will oversee the nomination process, ensure that all candidates meet the legal criteria, and officially list them on the ballot for the by-election.","html":"In this event, the IEC will oversee the nomination process, ensure that all candidates meet the legal criteria, and officially list them on the ballot for the by-election."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Subsequent to his resignation, Jacobs stated that his decision to leave the ANC and officially join the PA was motivated by a number of issues within the municipality which, in his opinion, the ANC continually failed to address.","html":"Subsequent to his resignation, Jacobs stated that his decision to leave the ANC and officially join the PA was motivated by a number of issues within the municipality which, in his opinion, the ANC continually failed to address."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"These issues include, inter alia, the alleged irregularities in the process of appointing certain employees, the awarding of certain tenders, and the outsourcing of municipal services.","html":"These issues include, inter alia, the alleged irregularities in the process of appointing certain employees, the awarding of certain tenders, and the outsourcing of municipal services."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Graaff-Reinet Advertiser is investigating Jacobs’ allegations and will report on them in due time."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"It has not yet been confirmed which parties or independent candidates will be contesting the by-election in Ward 7. It has, however, been confirmed that the IEC declared the vacancy during a Municipal Party Liaison Committee (MPLC) meeting last Thursday, 15 January, and that the by-election will officially be called on 6 February.","html":"It has not yet been confirmed which parties or independent candidates will be contesting the by-election in Ward 7. It has, however, been confirmed that the IEC declared the vacancy during a Municipal Party Liaison Committee (MPLC) meeting last Thursday, 15 January, and that the by-election will officially be called on 6 February."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Registration weekend has been scheduled for 31 January to 1 February, whereafter special votes will open on 2 March and close on 17 March, and the by-election will take place on 18 March.","html":"Registration weekend has been scheduled for 31 January to 1 February, whereafter special votes will open on 2 March and close on 17 March, and the by-election will take place on 18 March."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The DBNLM council comprises 24 councillors. Prior to Jacobs’ resignation, the ANC (11 councillors), with the supporting votes of the CSA (1) and EFF (1), generally controlled the decision-making power in council, as opposed to the DA (10) and FF+ (1).","html":"The DBNLM council comprises 24 councillors. Prior to Jacobs’ resignation, the ANC (11 councillors), with the supporting votes of the CSA (1) and EFF (1), generally controlled the decision-making power in council, as opposed to the DA (10) and FF+ (1)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"With Jacobs’ resignation, there may be a shift in the power dynamics within the municipality should a different party take control of Ward 7.","html":"With Jacobs’ resignation, there may be a shift in the power dynamics within the municipality should a different party take control of Ward 7."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Aberdeen Ward 7 heads to by-election after ANC councillor resigns
